message:
refactor(render): remove unused imports and silence dead code warnings

## Summary

Cleans up inspector code by removing unused constants from imports and prefixing unused variables with underscores. This eliminates compiler warnings without changing behavior.

## Changes

### 1. Remove Unused Imports (line 12)

**Removed:**
```rust
use scriptbots_core::{
    INPUT_SIZE,   // Removed
    OUTPUT_SIZE,  // Removed
    // ... other imports
};
```

**Why removed:**
- `INPUT_SIZE` and `OUTPUT_SIZE` were imported but never used in render code
- Sensor/output counts derived from runtime data (detail.sensors.len())
- Constants only needed in brain implementation, not visualization
- Removing reduces coupling to core's internal constants

**Verified unused:**
```bash
git grep -n "INPUT_SIZE" crates/scriptbots-render/
# No matches (was only in import)
git grep -n "OUTPUT_SIZE" crates/scriptbots-render/
# No matches (was only in import)
```

### 2. Prefix Unused Variables (lines 4336, 4343)

**Changed:**
```rust
// Before:
let sensors_preview: Vec<String> = detail.sensors...;
let outputs_preview: Vec<String> = detail.outputs...;

// After:
let _sensors_preview: Vec<String> = detail.sensors...;
let _outputs_preview: Vec<String> = detail.outputs...;
```

**Why these exist but are unused:**
- Originally used for text preview in inspector
- Replaced by visual bar charts (commit 2aa37dd)
- Left in code for potential future use (CSV export, debug mode, etc.)
- Underscore prefix suppresses warnings while keeping code ready

**Alternative considered:**
```rust
// Could delete entirely:
// let sensors_preview = ...
```
But keeping with `_` prefix is better because:
- Easy to remove underscore if needed later
- Shows intent (was used, might be used again)
- Documents what the computation does (preview formatting)

## Compiler Warnings Eliminated

**Before:**
```
warning: unused import: `INPUT_SIZE`
  --> crates/scriptbots-render/src/lib.rs:12:5
   |
12 |     INPUT_SIZE,
   |     ^^^^^^^^^^

warning: unused import: `OUTPUT_SIZE`
  --> crates/scriptbots-render/src/lib.rs:13:5
   |
13 |     OUTPUT_SIZE,
   |     ^^^^^^^^^^^

warning: unused variable: `sensors_preview`
  --> crates/scriptbots-render/src/lib.rs:4336:13
   |
4336 |         let sensors_preview: Vec<String> = detail
   |             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

warning: unused variable: `outputs_preview`
  --> crates/scriptbots-render/src/lib.rs:4343:13
   |
4343 |         let outputs_preview: Vec<String> = detail
   |             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

**After:**
```
(no warnings)
```

## Why This Matters

**Code quality:**
- Clean builds without warnings
- Easier to spot new issues (no noise)
- Shows code is maintained (warnings addressed)

**Documentation:**
- `_` prefix clearly marks intentionally unused code
- Future developers know these aren't bugs
- Preserves context about what data is available

**CI/CD:**
- Some CI configs fail on warnings
- Clean builds are faster (no warning output)
- Easier to enforce "zero warnings" policy

## When to Use `_` Prefix vs Deletion

**Use `_` prefix when:**
- Variable might be used in future feature
- Shows what data is available for debugging
- Complex computation worth preserving
- Example: `_sensors_preview` (might add CSV export later)

**Delete entirely when:**
- Code is truly obsolete (old algorithm, deprecated API)
- Never will be used again
- Example: old imports that were refactored away

**This commit uses `_` prefix** because:
- Preview strings might be useful for debugging
- Could add "copy to clipboard" feature using these
- Could add text-only fallback mode
- Low cost to keep (just variable binding)

## Testing

**Verified no behavior change:**
- Inspector renders identically ✓
- No new warnings ✓
- No new errors ✓
- Build time unchanged ✓

**Verified variables truly unused:**
```bash
git grep "sensors_preview" crates/scriptbots-render/
# Only shows the _sensors_preview definition

git grep "outputs_preview" crates/scriptbots-render/
# Only shows the _outputs_preview definition
```

## Related Work

**Part of inspector visualization improvements:**
- Commit 2aa37dd: Added visual bars (replaced text previews)
- Commit ffe987d: Added radar chart
- Commit 9774b72: Added sensor heat ring
- This commit: Cleanup after visualization changes

**Code quality progression:**
- Previous commits: Add features
- This commit: Clean up after features stabilized
- Pattern: Implement → Iterate → Clean up unused code

## Breaking Changes

None. Internal refactoring only:
- No API changes
- No behavior changes
- No performance changes
- Pure cleanup
